description_long: This sculpture depicts an elongated human hand and forearm, rendered in a stylized and upright pose with the fingers slightly spread and the palm facing outward. The surface is predominantly a weathered turquoise blue with patches of brownish rust and patina, suggesting it is made of aged metal, possibly bronze or copper. The texture is rough and mottled, with visible corrosion and pitting that give it an antique, industrial appearance. The hand is life-sized or slightly larger, with realistic anatomical details such as knuckles, fingernails, and subtle muscle contours. The base of the forearm is cylindrical and flat, allowing the sculpture to stand upright on a surface. The overall form is expressive, evoking a sense of gesture or communication.
description: A weathered turquoise-blue metal sculpture of an upright human hand and forearm, with realistic details and a rough, corroded surface.
description_view_0: Front view showing the palm and fingers of the hand, with the forearm extending downward and the surface's mottled patina clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the profile of the hand and the curvature of the fingers, with rust patches more prominent along the arm.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the hand and forearm, with the knuckles and finger joints visible and the patina continuing around the form.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the thumb and the side of the palm, with the elongated arm and weathered surface details.
attribute_location_context: No specific setting is visible; the sculpture could be placed in an urban plaza, park, or gallery.
